title: Self-rechargeable battery
abstract: The present invention describes about a self-rechargeable battery. The self-rechargeable battery comprises of an anode current collector, an anode layer, a cathode current collector, a cathode layer, a magnetic layer, a magnetic shield, an electrolyte and a separating membrane. The separating membrane separates the anode layer and the cathode layer. The separating membrane is positioned within the electrolyte. The battery is equipped with a magnetic layer to charge the battery thereby not requiring any external power supply to charge. The magnetic layer is positioned at the anode current collector. The present invention is advantageous as it increases the life span of the battery, provides high efficiency, and prevents the battery from over discharging as there is no need of any external input power supply.
claims: Claims im: 1. A self-rechargeable battery comprising: an anode current collector; an anode layer; a cathode current collector a cathode layer; an electrolyte; a separating membrane positioned within said electrolyte; a magnetic layer positioned at said anode current collector to drive chargeable atoms; and a magnetic shield positioned adjacent to said magnetic layer. 2. The self-rechargeable battery as claimed in claim 1, wherein material of said anode layer may comprise graphite, aluminum, magnesium and the like and, wherein material of said cathode layer may comprise metal oxides of cobalt, manganese, nickel, phosphate and the like. 3. The self-rechargeable battery as claimed in claim 1, wherein said magnetic layer may comprise any magnetic source that propagates magnetic field in desired direction and, wherein said magnetic source may be an anisotropic or modified isotropic and the like. 4. The self-rechargeable battery as claimed in claim 1, wherein said magnetic layer can also be positioned either at said cathode current collector or in between said anode and cathode current collectors with certain modifications and thereof. 5. The self-rechargeable battery as claimed in claim 1, wherein said chargeable atoms can be either lithium or sodium and the like based on the type of the battery used. 6. The self-rechargeable battery as claimed in claim 1, wherein said magnetic shield positioned adjacent to said magnetic layer blocks the magnetic field in undesirable direction and avoids magnetic flux leakages. 7. The self-rechargeable battery as claimed in claim 1, wherein type of said battery can be applicable for industrial purposes, domestic purposes, electronic gadgets, automobiles and the like. 8. A method for charging and discharging of a self-rechargeable battery comprising: attracting paramagnetic chargeable atoms towards an anode layer from a cathode layer by means of a magnetic layer at said anode current collector; intercalating said chargeable atoms on said anode layer; dissociating said chargeable atoms into ions and electrons at said anode layer due to electrode potential; passing of electrons from said anode current collector to said cathode current collector through external circuit; repelling said diamagnetic ions towards said cathode layer through electrolyte layer; and forming of chargeable atoms on said cathode layer due to chemical bond between said ions and said electrons.
